etymology.description: Depicts a stylized hand with bent wrist -> original 'elbow' 肘 (zhǒu) meaning -> associated with nine as metaphorical bumping up against ten
etymology.interpretation: 九 originally depicted the elbow, symbolizing movement akin to pulling back in momentum, which culturally reflects nearing a complete cycle at ten. Later, 九 came to symbolize the number nine, a fist bumping up against ten, the last count on one's fingers.

examples.chinese: 今天是九号(今天是九號)。
她有九个苹果(她有九個蘋果)。
我们九点钟出发(我們九點鐘出發)，去探望朋友(去探望朋友)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen jiǔ diǎn zhōng chūfā, qù tànwàng péngyǒu.
examples.english: We will leave at nine o'clock to visit a friend.
